overview.tagline: A warm, walkable borough with small-town character and strong community roots
overview.description: Midland Park is a tight-knit residential borough in northwestern Bergen County known for its friendly atmosphere, well-maintained neighborhoods, and active civic life. Originally part of Ridgewood, the borough was incorporated in 1894 and has retained its small-town charm while offering convenient access to shopping and transportation. With a compact downtown along Godwin Avenue, strong public schools, and a calendar full of community events, Midland Park is a popular choice for families seeking a welcoming suburban environment.
highlights: Charming downtown along Godwin Avenue with local shops and restaurants
Annual community events including Midland Park Day
Easy access to Route 17 and Route 208
Strong volunteer fire department and community organizations

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County periodically conducts revaluations to align assessed values with market conditions.
realEstate.recentTrends: Midland Park has seen steady price appreciation in recent years, driven by demand from families attracted to the school district and community feel. Homes in good condition near the downtown tend to sell quickly. The market remains competitive with limited inventory.
neighborhoodNotes: Godwin Avenue corridor — Walking distance to downtown shops and restaurants
West Midland Park — Quiet residential streets near Midland Park High School
East side — Close to Route 17 shopping and Ridgewood border
North end — Larger lots near the Waldwick border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided weekly by the Borough of Midland Park Department of Public Works.
details: Trash pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Containers must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Maximum weight per container is 50 lbs with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with DPW
Holiday collection schedule posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastics #1-#7) collected biweekly
Paper and cardboard collected on alternate weeks
Recycling containers provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally from spring through fall
Special recycling events held at the DPW yard throughout the year

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service in Midland Park is provided by Ridgewood Water. Sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Ridgewood Water handles water supply and billing
Sewer maintenance is managed by the Borough DPW
Water quality reports available annually from Ridgewood Water
Emergency water line issues handled 24/7
Sewer connection permits required for new construction

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department oversees construction permits, zoning applications, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, renovations, and additions
Zoning Board of Adjustment reviews variance applications
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of occupancy required for all new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages municipal parking in the downtown area and commuter lots.
details: Free parking available in municipal lots along Godwin Avenue
Commuter parking permits available for NJ Transit bus riders
Street parking subject to posted time limits in the downtown area
Overnight parking restrictions enforced borough-wide
Snow emergency parking rules in effect during winter storms

additionalServices.title: Midland Park Memorial Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, and community programs.
details: Located at 250 Godwin Avenue
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Recreational programs and activities for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues including baseball, soccer, and basketball
Summer recreation programs and camps
Senior programs and activities
Community events throughout the year

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Midland Park enforces noise regulations to preserve the quiet residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction noise on Sundays or holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Loud music and amplified sound prohibited after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in accordance with borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept mowed and maintained
Properties must be free of debris and junk
Fences and structures must be kept in good repair
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow within 24 hours after a storm
Vacant properties must be registered and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Pet ownership in Midland Park is subject to licensing and leash requirements.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed annually with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Barking dogs that disturb neighbors are subject to complaints and fines
Cat licensing is required

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking rules are enforced to maintain order and safety on borough streets.
keyRules: Overnight parking on streets is prohibited from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
Vehicles must be moved during snow emergencies
No parking within 25 feet of intersections
Commercial vehicles over 10,000 lbs prohibited from parking on residential streets overnight
Parking violations subject to fines and towing

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties in Midland Park must comply with registration and inspection requirements.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of habitability required before renting a unit
Landlords responsible for maintaining properties to code standards
Short-term rentals (such as Airbnb) subject to local zoning regulations
Multi-family properties subject to periodic inspections

other.title: Fence Regulations
other.summary: Fences must comply with height and placement requirements.
keyRules: Residential fences limited to 6 feet in rear yards
Front yard fences limited to 4 feet
Fences must be approved by the Building Department
Finished side of fence must face outward toward neighboring property
other.source: Borough of Midland Park Zoning Code

publicSchools.name: Godwin School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving all borough students in early grades

publicSchools.name: Highland School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school with strong academic and extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Midland Park Jr./Sr. High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior-senior high school; also serves students from Ho-Ho-Kus through a sending-receiving relationship

privateSchools.name: Eastern Christian School
privateSchools.notes: Christian school with campuses in Midland Park and North Haledon

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 miles

whyMoveHere: Small-town atmosphere with strong community spirit
Affordable by Bergen County standards with good school options
Walkable downtown with local shops and restaurants
Easy access to Route 17 and Route 208 for commuting
Active recreational programs and community events

commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus route 175 provides direct service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. Some residents also drive to nearby train stations in Waldwick or Ridgewood for rail service.

costOfLiving.summary: Midland Park offers relatively affordable housing for Bergen County while still providing access to excellent schools and amenities.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Above average, consistent with northern Bergen County suburban communities
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Significantly above the national average due to property taxes and housing costs

pros: Strong sense of community and active civic life
More affordable than neighboring Ridgewood and Glen Rock
Good public schools with small class sizes
Low crime rate and safe neighborhoods
cons: No direct train service — bus commute to NYC can be long
Property taxes remain high relative to home values
Small commercial district with limited shopping
Some homes are older and may require updates

restaurants.name: The Park Pub
restaurants.description: Popular neighborhood pub serving American fare, burgers, and craft beers in a casual setting.

restaurants.name: Bianca's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Family-friendly Italian restaurant known for homemade pasta and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: Midland Brew House
restaurants.description: Craft brewery and restaurant featuring local beers and a lively atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Villa Amalfi
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian dining with a warm ambiance and extensive wine list.

parks.name: Midland Park Municipal Park
parks.description: Central community park with playgrounds, sports fields, and picnic areas.

parks.name: Wortendyke Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with open green space, walking paths, and a basketball court.

parks.name: Lions Club Park
parks.description: Small park maintained by the local Lions Club with playground equipment and benches.

events.name: Midland Park Day
events.description: Annual borough-wide celebration featuring live music, food vendors, games, and a community parade.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony in the downtown area with caroling and refreshments.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Community parade honoring veterans with marching bands and civic organizations.

attractions.name: Midland Park Downtown
attractions.description: Walkable downtown along Godwin Avenue featuring local shops, restaurants, and services.

attractions.name: Eastern Christian School Campus
attractions.description: Historic Christian school campus that has been a cornerstone of the community since 1892.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Midland Park Borough Hall, 280 Godwin Avenue

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
